notes:
vMLX 1.5.65

Highlights:
- Moves the MiniMax-M3 compatibility release to a new updater-visible version after 1.5.64 shipped.
- MiniMax-M3 auto-detection keeps paged cache off, JIT off, generic TurboQuant KV off, native MSA/Lightning sparse cache first-class, SSD prefix cache on, and MiniMax reasoning/tool parsers selected.
- Fixes and verifies UI/API paths for MiniMax-M3 reasoning on/off/auto, visible content emission, tool calls, Responses, Chat Completions, Anthropic, Ollama, streaming, VL image input, and prefix-cache reuse telemetry.
- Adds clearer cache/memory UI behavior for paged cache capacity and ignored memory fields so CLI and GUI users can see effective cache pressure.
- Keeps Gemma 4 VL scoped rows in the release gate and preserves Gemma mixed-SWA/TurboQuant KV boundaries.

Verification:
- 1.5.65 source stamps are aligned across package.json, package-lock.json, pyproject.toml, and vmlx_engine.__version__.
- Scoped 1.5.65 MM3/Gemma preflight passed before packaging.
- Bundled Python verification passed for both Sequoia and Tahoe builds, including vmlx_engine 1.5.65, jang-tools, MLX, mlx-lm, mlx-vlm, MiniMax/Gemma-related imports, and bundled source parity checks.
- Sequoia and Tahoe DMGs are Developer ID signed by ShieldStack LLC (55KGF2S5AY), Apple notarized, stapled, Gatekeeper accepted, and verified by the release DMG verifier.

Known follow-up:
- Broad non-scoped model-family red rows remain tracked for the next release.
- Additional MM3 diagnostic cache-off CLI proof and extended cache-mode UI matrix remain next-release hardening rows.
- Gemma audio is outside this scoped release; Gemma VL remains the scoped media target.
